name: SERVICE_NOW__LIST_INCIDENTS
description: Retrieve a list of incidents from ServiceNow with optional filters, sorting, and pagination. Useful for dashboards, reporting, and monitoring open issues.

query.description: Query parameters for filtering, sorting, and paginating incidents.

sysparm_query.description: Encoded query string to filter and sort results. Use '^' as AND operator. Append '^ORDERBYfield' or '^ORDERBYDESCfield' for sorting. e.g., 'state=1^priority=1^ORDERBYDESCopened_at'.

sysparm_limit.description: Maximum number of records to return. Default is 10, max is 10000.

sysparm_fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return. Omit to return all fields.

sysparm_display_value.description: Controls whether to return display values or raw values. Use 'true' for human-readable labels.

name: SERVICE_NOW__GET_INCIDENT
description: Retrieve a single incident record by its sys_id from ServiceNow, including all field details.

path.description: Path parameters to identify the incident.

sys_id.description: The unique sys_id of the incident record.

query.description: Optional query parameters.

sysparm_display_value.description: Return display values instead of raw values.

sysparm_fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return.

name: SERVICE_NOW__CREATE_INCIDENT
description: Create a new incident record in ServiceNow. Used to log new IT issues, outages, or service disruptions.

body.description: The incident record fields to set on creation.

examples: VPN connectivity issue for all remote users
All users in the London office are unable to connect to the VPN since 09:00 AM GMT.

assignment_group.description: sys_id or name of the group to assign this incident to.

assigned_to.description: sys_id or username of the user to assign the incident to.

caller_id.description: sys_id or username of the person who reported the incident.

cmdb_ci.description: sys_id of the Configuration Item affected by this incident.

name: SERVICE_NOW__UPDATE_INCIDENT
description: Update an existing incident record in ServiceNow. Used to change state, add notes, reassign, or close an incident.

path.description: Path parameters to identify the incident.

sys_id.description: The unique sys_id of the incident to update.

body.description: Fields to update on the incident record.

work_notes.description: Internal work notes visible only to agents.
examples: Escalated to network team. Investigating router config.

comments.description: Public comments visible to the caller/customer.
examples: We are actively working on this issue. ETA for resolution is 2 hours.

close_code.description: Resolution code when closing or resolving an incident.

close_notes.description: Resolution notes when closing or resolving the incident.
examples: Restarted the VPN concentrator. All users reconnected successfully.

assigned_to.description: sys_id or username of the user to reassign the incident to.

assignment_group.description: sys_id or name of the group to reassign this incident to.

name: SERVICE_NOW__LIST_CHANGE_REQUESTS
description: Retrieve a list of change requests from ServiceNow with optional filters. Used to track planned changes to IT infrastructure and services.

query.description: Query parameters for filtering and paginating change requests.

sysparm_query.description: Encoded query string to filter change requests. e.g., 'state=assess^type=normal' for normal changes awaiting assessment.

sysparm_limit.description: Maximum number of records to return.

sysparm_fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return.

sysparm_display_value.description: Return display values instead of raw values.

name: SERVICE_NOW__GET_CHANGE_REQUEST
description: Retrieve a single change request record by its sys_id from ServiceNow.

path.description: Path parameters to identify the change request.

sys_id.description: The unique sys_id of the change request record.

query.description: Optional query parameters.

sysparm_display_value.description: Return display values instead of raw values.

sysparm_fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return.

name: SERVICE_NOW__CREATE_CHANGE_REQUEST
description: Create a new change request in ServiceNow. Supports Standard, Normal, and Emergency change types.

body.description: The change request record fields to set on creation.

examples: Upgrade database server from PostgreSQL 14 to 16

description.description: Detailed description and justification for the change.

start_date.description: Planned start date/time of the change in format 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'.

end_date.description: Planned end date/time of the change in format 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'.

assignment_group.description: Name or sys_id of the group responsible for implementing the change.

assigned_to.description: sys_id or username of the person responsible for implementing the change.

cmdb_ci.description: sys_id of the Configuration Item being changed.

examples: Required for security compliance. Current version has known CVEs.

implementation_plan.description: Step-by-step plan for implementing the change.

backout_plan.description: Plan for reverting the change if issues are encountered.

test_plan.description: Plan for testing the change after implementation.

name: SERVICE_NOW__UPDATE_CHANGE_REQUEST
description: Update an existing change request in ServiceNow. Used to update state, add implementation notes, or close a change.

path.description: Path parameters to identify the change request.

sys_id.description: The unique sys_id of the change request to update.

body.description: Fields to update on the change request.

examples: Database upgrade completed successfully. All services verified.

close_code.description: Close code when closing the change.

close_notes.description: Notes summarizing the outcome of the change.

start_date.description: Actual or revised start date in format 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'.

end_date.description: Actual or revised end date in format 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'.

name: SERVICE_NOW__LIST_PROBLEMS
description: Retrieve a list of problem records from ServiceNow. Problems represent the root cause of one or more incidents.

query.description: Query parameters for filtering and paginating problem records.

sysparm_limit.description: Maximum number of records to return.

sysparm_fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return.

sysparm_display_value.description: Return display values instead of raw values.

name: SERVICE_NOW__GET_PROBLEM
description: Retrieve a single problem record by its sys_id from ServiceNow.

path.description: Path parameters to identify the problem record.

sys_id.description: The unique sys_id of the problem record.

query.description: Optional query parameters.

sysparm_display_value.description: Return display values instead of raw values.

name: SERVICE_NOW__CREATE_PROBLEM
description: Create a new problem record in ServiceNow to track root cause investigation of recurring or major incidents.

body.description: Fields for the new problem record.

examples: Recurring VPN disconnections for remote workers

description.description: Detailed description of the problem and its impact.

assignment_group.description: Name or sys_id of the group assigned to investigate the problem.

assigned_to.description: sys_id or username of the person assigned to investigate.

known_error.description: Set to true if the root cause has been identified (known error).

workaround.description: Temporary workaround to mitigate the problem until resolved.
examples: Users should reconnect to the backup VPN endpoint.

name: SERVICE_NOW__LIST_SERVICE_REQUESTS
description: Retrieve a list of service catalog requests (sc_request) from ServiceNow. Used to track requests submitted through the service catalog.

query.description: Query parameters for filtering and paginating service requests.

sysparm_query.description: Encoded query string to filter service requests.

sysparm_limit.description: Maximum number of records to return.

sysparm_fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return.

sysparm_display_value.description: Return display values instead of raw values.

name: SERVICE_NOW__GET_REQUEST_ITEMS
description: Retrieve all requested items (RITM - sc_req_item) associated with a service request. Each item represents a catalog item ordered by the user.

query.description: Query parameters for filtering request items.

sysparm_query.description: Encoded query string. Use 'request={request_sys_id}' to filter items for a specific request.

sysparm_limit.description: Maximum number of records to return.

sysparm_display_value.description: Return display values instead of raw values.

sysparm_fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return.

name: SERVICE_NOW__LIST_CMDB_CIS
description: Retrieve configuration items (CIs) from the ServiceNow CMDB. Used to discover and audit IT assets such as servers, applications, and network devices.

query.description: Query parameters for filtering and paginating CMDB configuration items.

sysparm_limit.description: Maximum number of records to return.

sysparm_fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return.

sysparm_display_value.description: Return display values instead of raw values.

name: SERVICE_NOW__GET_CMDB_CI
description: Retrieve a single Configuration Item (CI) from the ServiceNow CMDB by its sys_id.

path.description: Path parameters to identify the CI.

sys_id.description: The unique sys_id of the configuration item.

query.description: Optional query parameters.

sysparm_display_value.description: Return display values instead of raw values.

sysparm_fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return.

name: SERVICE_NOW__LIST_USERS
description: Retrieve a list of users from ServiceNow's sys_user table. Used for looking up user accounts, roles, and profile information.

query.description: Query parameters for filtering and paginating users.

sysparm_limit.description: Maximum number of records to return.

sysparm_fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return.

sysparm_display_value.description: Return display values instead of raw values.

name: SERVICE_NOW__GET_USER
description: Retrieve a single user record from ServiceNow by sys_id. Used to look up user details such as name, email, department, and manager.

path.description: Path parameters to identify the user.

sys_id.description: The unique sys_id of the user record.

query.description: Optional query parameters.

sysparm_display_value.description: Return display values instead of raw values.

sysparm_fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return.

name: SERVICE_NOW__LIST_KNOWLEDGE_ARTICLES
description: Retrieve knowledge base articles from ServiceNow. Used to surface self-service documentation, FAQs, and known error workarounds.

query.description: Query parameters for filtering and paginating knowledge articles.

sysparm_query.description: Encoded query to filter knowledge articles.

sysparm_limit.description: Maximum number of records to return.

sysparm_fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return.

sysparm_display_value.description: Return display values instead of raw values.

name: SERVICE_NOW__GET_KNOWLEDGE_ARTICLE
description: Retrieve a single knowledge base article from ServiceNow by its sys_id, including full article body content.

path.description: Path parameters to identify the knowledge article.

sys_id.description: The unique sys_id of the knowledge article.

query.description: Optional query parameters.

sysparm_display_value.description: Return display values instead of raw values.

sysparm_fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return.

name: SERVICE_NOW__SEARCH_KNOWLEDGE_BASE
description: Search the ServiceNow Knowledge Base using the full-text search API. Returns relevant articles matching keywords or phrases.

query.description: Search parameters for the knowledge base.

sysparm_query.description: Encoded query with search terms using CONTAINS or LIKE operators on the short_description or text field.

sysparm_limit.description: Maximum number of articles to return.

sysparm_fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return.

sysparm_display_value.description: Return display values instead of raw values.

name: SERVICE_NOW__ADD_COMMENT_TO_RECORD
description: Add a public comment or internal work note to any ServiceNow task record (incident, change request, problem, RITM, etc.).

path.description: Path parameters to identify the target record.

table_name.description: The name of the ServiceNow table containing the record.

sys_id.description: The unique sys_id of the record to add a comment to.

body.description: Comment or work note content to add.

comments.description: Public comment visible to the caller/customer and agents.
examples: We are investigating the issue and will provide an update within 30 minutes.

work_notes.description: Internal work note visible only to agents and not to the end user.
examples: Checked with the network team. The issue is related to a misconfigured firewall rule.

name: SERVICE_NOW__QUERY_TABLE
description: Generic API to query any ServiceNow table by name. Use this for tables not covered by specific functions, such as custom tables, approvals (sysapproval_approver), or SLAs (task_sla).

path.description: Path parameters specifying the target table.

table_name.description: The name of the ServiceNow table to query.

query.description: Query parameters for filtering, selecting fields, and paginating results.

sysparm_query.description: Encoded query string to filter and sort records. Use '^' as AND operator. Append '^ORDERBYfield' or '^ORDERBYDESCfield' for sorting. e.g., 'active=true^state=requested^ORDERBYDESCsys_created_on'.

sysparm_limit.description: Maximum number of records to return.

sysparm_fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return. Omit to return all fields.

sysparm_display_value.description: Return display values instead of raw values.

name: SERVICE_NOW__GET_AGGREGATE_STATS
description: Retrieve aggregate statistics (count, sum, avg, min, max) over any ServiceNow table. Useful for reporting, KPIs, and dashboards — e.g., count of open P1 incidents, average resolution time.

path.description: Path parameters specifying the target table.

table_name.description: The ServiceNow table to aggregate over.

query.description: Query parameters for aggregation.

sysparm_query.description: Encoded query to filter records before aggregation.

sysparm_count.description: Set to true to include a record count in the response.

sysparm_sum_fields.description: Comma-separated list of numeric fields to sum.

sysparm_avg_fields.description: Comma-separated list of numeric fields to average.

sysparm_min_fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to find minimum values for.

sysparm_max_fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to find maximum values for.

sysparm_group_by.description: Field to group aggregated results by.

sysparm_display_value.description: Return display values instead of raw values.

name: SERVICE_NOW__LIST_ATTACHMENTS
description: List all attachments linked to a specific ServiceNow record such as an incident, change request, or problem. Returns file name, size, content type, and sys_id for each attachment.

query.description: Query parameters to filter attachments by record.

sysparm_query.description: Filter attachments by table and record sys_id. e.g., table_name=incident^table_sys_id=1234abcd1234abcd1234abcd1234abcd

sysparm_limit.description: Maximum number of attachments to return.

sysparm_fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return.

sysparm_display_value.description: Return display values instead of raw values.

name: SERVICE_NOW__GET_ATTACHMENT_METADATA
description: Get metadata of a specific attachment by its sys_id. Returns file name, size, content type, and which record it is attached to. Use LIST_ATTACHMENTS first to find the sys_id.

path.description: Path parameters to identify the attachment.

sys_id.description: The unique sys_id of the attachment record.

name: SERVICE_NOW__DOWNLOAD_ATTACHMENT
description: Download the binary file content of an attachment by its sys_id. Returns the raw file with its original content type such as PDF, PNG, or text. Use GET_ATTACHMENT_METADATA first to find the sys_id.

path.description: Path parameters to identify the attachment to download.

sys_id.description: The unique sys_id of the attachment to download.

name: SERVICE_NOW__UPLOAD_ATTACHMENT
description: Upload a file attachment to any ServiceNow record such as an incident, change request, or problem. Use this to attach error logs, screenshots, or documents directly to a record.

query.description: Query parameters specifying which record to attach the file to.

table_name.description: The ServiceNow table name of the record to attach the file to.

table_sys_id.description: The sys_id of the specific record to attach the file to.

file_name.description: The name of the file being uploaded including extension.

content_type.description: MIME type of the file being uploaded.

content.description: The file content as a base64-encoded string for binary files, or plain text for text files.

name: SERVICE_NOW__DELETE_ATTACHMENT
description: Permanently delete a specific attachment from a ServiceNow record by its sys_id. Use LIST_ATTACHMENTS first to confirm the correct sys_id before deleting.

path.description: Path parameters to identify the attachment to delete.

sys_id.description: The unique sys_id of the attachment to permanently delete.

name: SERVICE_NOW__CREATE_HR_CASE
description: Create a new HR case in ServiceNow HR Service Delivery. Used to log HR requests such as onboarding, offboarding, benefits, payroll queries, or employee relations issues.

body.description: Fields to set on the new HR case record.

short_description.description: Brief summary of the HR case.
examples: Employee relocation request for John Smith
Benefits enrollment issue for new joiner

description.description: Detailed description of the HR case and what the employee needs.
examples: Employee John Smith is relocating from London to New York and needs HR support for the transition including visa and tax documentation.

subject_person.description: sys_id or username of the employee this HR case is about.

hr_service.description: sys_id or name of the HR service this case belongs to.

assignment_group.description: Name or sys_id of the HR team to assign this case to.

assigned_to.description: sys_id or username of the HR agent to assign this case to.

opened_for.description: sys_id or username of the employee who the case is opened for.

name: SERVICE_NOW__UPDATE_HR_CASE
description: Update an existing HR case in ServiceNow. Used to change state, reassign, add comments, or close an HR case.

path.description: Path parameters to identify the HR case.

sys_id.description: The unique sys_id of the HR case to update.

body.description: Fields to update on the HR case.

state.description: Updated state of the HR case.

short_description.description: Updated summary of the HR case.
examples: Updated: Employee relocation request for John Smith

description.description: Updated detailed description of the case.

assignment_group.description: Name or sys_id of the HR team to reassign this case to.

assigned_to.description: sys_id or username of the HR agent to reassign to.

comments.description: Public comment visible to the employee.
examples: We have received your request and are processing it. Expected resolution within 3 business days.

work_notes.description: Internal work note visible only to HR agents.
examples: Escalated to senior HR advisor. Awaiting approval from line manager.

close_notes.description: Resolution notes when closing the case.
examples: Relocation package approved and communicated to employee. Case resolved.

name: SERVICE_NOW__GET_HR_CASE
description: Retrieve full details of a specific HR case by its sys_id including state, subject employee, HR service, assigned team, comments, and work notes.

path.description: Path parameters to identify the HR case.

sys_id.description: The unique sys_id of the HR case to retrieve.

query.description: Optional query parameters.

sysparm_display_value.description: Return human-readable display values instead of raw sys_ids.

sysparm_fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return. Omit to return all fields.

name: SERVICE_NOW__ADD_ATTACHMENT_TO_HR_CASE
description: Upload and attach a file to an HR case in ServiceNow. Use this to attach supporting documents such as offer letters, medical certificates, payslips, or ID documents to an HR case.

query.description: Query parameters specifying which HR case to attach the file to.

table_name.description: The ServiceNow table for HR cases. Always use sn_hr_core_case for HR cases.

table_sys_id.description: The sys_id of the HR case to attach the file to.

file_name.description: The name of the file being uploaded including its extension.

content_type.description: MIME type of the file being uploaded.

content.description: The file content as a base64-encoded string for binary files such as PDFs and images, or plain text for text files.
